content.full_text:
1230 J STREET
SAcRAMENTo, CALTFoRNIA 958 l4
September L, L992
Ms. Maryann Kvasager
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, California 95804
Dear Ma ryann :
Pursuant to our telephone conversations, I am writing to
seek informal advice on behalf of Secretary of State March
Fong Eu and her former political 
committee regarding the
proper method of reporting receipts associated with
commi t tee- sponso red trips. 
I have received ora] aalvice from
commission staff over the years regarding such reporting
procedures but I have been unable to locate written responses
to inquiries. 
This current inquiry arises in the context of
the routine audit of political 
committees by the Franchise Tax
Boa rcl .In 1988, the Frienals of March Fong Eu (now terminated)
sponsoreal a trip to promote California trade anal investment
and to raise campaign funds ' 
The campaign contracted with a
travel agency in oriler to handle the arrangements for the
trip. 
Each traveler paid the travel agency $3,300 for the
travel package. The actual cost of provicling the trip by the
travel agency was $2,250. The fair market value of the trip
was deemed to be $2,500. The travel agency rebated to the
campaign S1,050 per traveler. 
of this amount, the campaign
reported $800 as being a contribution from the traveler and
$250 as being a contribution from the travel agency. This
method of reporting was consistent with the oral advice
received from commission staff and with the alef inition of a
"contribution" as being a payment "except to the extent that
fu11 and adequate consideration is receivecl".
I should very much appreciate receiving your informal
aclvice as to whether the cornrnittee properly reported these
transactions under the circumstances set forth.
Sincerely,
/-/n'
AN{HONY L.

Aucli to r , Eranchise Tax Board

sections.question: Whether the committee properly reported receipt transactions from a 1988 committee-sponsored trip to promote California trade and investment and raise campaign funds, where travelers paid $3,300 to a travel agency, the actual cost was $2,250, fair market value was $2,500, and the travel agency rebated $1,050 per traveler (reported as $800 contribution from traveler and $250 from travel agency).
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor seeks informal advice on behalf of Secretary of State March Fong Eu's former political committee regarding whether receipts from a committee-sponsored 1988 trip were properly reported, where the campaign received rebates from a travel agency that were allocated between traveler and travel agency contributions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter TO the FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. No conclusion/advice section is present in this document. The letter requests informal advice on contribution reporting from committee-sponsored trip transactions. Minor OCR issues present (e.g., 'aalvice' for 'advice', 'commi t tee' for 'committee').

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether the committee properly reported receipt transactions from a 1988 committee-sponsored trip to promote California trade and investment and raise campaign funds, where travelers paid $3,300 to a travel agency, the actual cost was $2,250, fair market value was $2,500, and the travel agency rebated $1,050 per traveler (reported as $800 contribution from traveler and $250 from travel agency).

I have received ora] aalvice from commission staff over the years regarding such reporting procedures but I have been unable to locate written responses to inquiries. This current inquiry arises in the context of the routine audit of political committees by the Franchise Tax Boa rcl .In 1988, the Frienals of March Fong Eu (now terminated) sponsoreal a trip to promote California trade anal investment and to raise campaign funds ' The campaign contracted with a travel agency in oriler to handle the arrangements for the trip. Each traveler paid the travel agency $3,300 for the travel package. The actual cost of provicling the trip by the travel agency was $2,250. The fair market value of the trip was deemed to be $2,500. The travel agency rebated to the campaign S1,050 per traveler. of this amount, the campaign reported $800 as being a contribution from the traveler and $250 as being a contribution from the travel agency. This method of reporting was consistent with the oral advice received from commission staff and with the alef inition of a "contribution" as being a payment "except to the extent that fu11 and adequate consideration is receivecl". embedding.summary: Anthony L. Miller requests informal FPPC advice on behalf of Secretary of State March Fong Eu's terminated political committee regarding the proper reporting method for receipts from a 1988 committee-sponsored trip, where travelers paid $3,300 but the travel agency rebated $1,050 per traveler that was reported as contributions.
